skill.name: bamboohr-automation
skill.description: Automate BambooHR employee, time-off, benefits, dependent, and profile update workflows through Rube MCP.
skill.summary: Run BambooHR HR workflows through Rube MCP with current tool schemas and permission-aware steps.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution, weak-crypto, and reconnaissance findings are false positives caused by Markdown backticks, ordinary HR text, and workflow documentation. The real risk is medium because the skill uses an external MCP endpoint to access sensitive BambooHR employee, dependent, benefits, and write-capable HR operations.

medium_findings.title: Sensitive HR Data Access Through External MCP
medium_findings.description: The skill directs users to configure Rube MCP at an external endpoint and use it for BambooHR workflows. The documented data includes employee PII, dependents, and benefit coverage, so misuse or overbroad permissions could expose sensitive HR records.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly names an external MCP endpoint and warns that employee and dependent records contain PII. This confirms a real data-handling risk, although it appears aligned with the skill purpose rather than malicious.

medium_findings.title: Write-Capable HR Workflows Require Guardrails
medium_findings.description: The skill documents BambooHR actions that create or update time-off requests and employee profile fields. These workflows can change HR records and should require user confirmation, correct role permissions, and verification of current values before execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented tool sequences include create, update, approve, deny, and employee update operations. The surrounding permission notes show these are legitimate but high-impact HR actions.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged many Ruby or shell backtick execution patterns. In context, these are Markdown inline-code markers and fenced workflow examples around BambooHR tool names and parameters, not executable Ruby or shell code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: SKILL.md is documentation and the flagged backticks surround tool names, parameter names, or numbered pseudocode. No executable script or shell interpolation is present in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Text Matches Only
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings do not correspond to cryptographic code. The cited lines contain the skill description and sensitive-data warnings, not hashing, encryption, or cryptographic algorithm usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations contain natural language about BambooHR and PII. I did not find cryptographic functions, algorithms, keys, or encoded payloads in those lines.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Findings Are Workflow Language
low_findings.description: The system and network reconnaissance alerts are false positives. The cited lines describe checking employee values, extracting BambooHR IDs from API results, permissions, and rate limits for normal HR automation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text is operational guidance for BambooHR IDs and permissions. I did not find host probing, port scanning, environment discovery, or network enumeration instructions.

content.value_statement: HR teams need reliable ways to handle repetitive BambooHR tasks without guessing tool parameters. This skill provides workflow guidance for employee lookup, time-off, benefits, dependents, and profile updates through Rube MCP.
actual_capabilities: Guides setup of Rube MCP and BambooHR connection checks.
Lists and retrieves BambooHR employee directory and profile details.
Tracks recently changed employees for sync and audit workflows.
Supports time-off type lookup, balance checks, requests, and status updates.
Documents employee profile update steps with current-value verification.
Covers dependent lookup and benefit coverage review workflows.
limitations: Requires an active Rube MCP connection and BambooHR authentication.
Access depends on BambooHR account features and API permissions.
Sensitive employee data must be handled under the user organization policy.
Tool schemas can change, so the skill requires searching current schemas first.

use_cases.title: Employee Directory Lookup
use_cases.description: Find employees, resolve numeric employee IDs, and retrieve selected profile fields for routine HR support.

use_cases.title: Time-Off Request Processing
use_cases.description: Check time-off types, balances, and request status before creating or updating BambooHR time-off requests.

use_cases.title: HR System Synchronization
use_cases.description: Use BambooHR changed-employee endpoints to support incremental sync workflows with downstream systems.

prompt_templates.title: Find an Employee
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the BambooHR automation skill to find the employee ID for [employee name]. Search the current Rube tool schemas first.

prompt_templates.title: Check Time-Off Balance
prompt_templates.prompt: Check the available time-off balance for employee [employee ID] and time-off type [type name]. Confirm the needed type ID before checking balances.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare an Employee Update
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the current BambooHR profile for employee [employee ID], then prepare an update for [field changes]. Ask for confirmation before making changes.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Incremental Sync
prompt_templates.prompt: Build an incremental BambooHR sync plan since [timestamp]. Retrieve changed employee IDs, identify required fields, and describe the downstream update order.

output_examples.input: Find the employee record for Jordan Lee and show department and job title.
output_examples.output: The response identifies matching employees, selects the correct employee ID, and returns the requested department and job title fields.

output_examples.input: Create a vacation request for employee 42 from July 10 to July 12.
output_examples.output: The response checks available time-off types, confirms the vacation type ID, verifies dates and employee ID, then asks before submitting the request.

output_examples.input: Sync employee changes since yesterday at midnight UTC.
output_examples.output: The response uses the changed-employee workflow, lists changed employee IDs, and outlines follow-up profile retrieval for each changed record.

best_practices: Search Rube tool schemas before every workflow because BambooHR tool parameters can change.
Request only the employee fields needed for the task to reduce sensitive data exposure.
Confirm user intent before creating requests, updating employees, or changing approval status.
anti_patterns: Do not retrieve full employee profiles when a limited field list is enough.
Do not update employee records before checking the current value and confirming the change.
Do not store or share dependent, benefits, or SSN-related data outside approved systems.

faq.question: Does this skill connect directly to BambooHR?
faq.answer: No. It guides BambooHR actions through Rube MCP, which must already be connected and authenticated.

faq.question: Can it update employee records?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents update workflows, but users should confirm current values and permissions before any change.

faq.question: Can it approve time-off requests?
faq.answer: It documents the update workflow for request status changes. BambooHR permissions determine whether approval is allowed.

faq.question: Does it handle sensitive HR data?
faq.answer: Yes. Employee, dependent, and benefits data can contain PII and must be handled under organization policy.

faq.question: Why does it search tools first?
faq.answer: Rube tool schemas may change. Searching first helps use current parameters and reduces failed requests.

faq.question: Is this safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium risk warning because it uses an external MCP service for sensitive HR data and write-capable workflows.
